commonMistakes: Treating request memoization and cross-request caching as the same thing.
Mutating data without invalidating affected entries.
Marking a large route tree as client code when only a small interaction needs it.
Confusing React Server Components with server-side rendering.

contentAuthority.canonical: New JS2Next Release 1 curriculum and independently authored content.
contentAuthority.courseTranscripts: Coverage and sequencing research only; never learner-facing authority.
contentAuthority.legacyProjectContent: Supplementary internal reference only; excluded from runtime unless explicitly re-authored and approved.

learningObjectives.text: Trace and explain request memoization using a new example and justify the result.
Explain data cache using a new example and justify the result.
Explain route cache using a new example and justify the result.
Explain revalidation considerations using a new example and justify the result.

mentalModel: Draw a decision map: **Request memoization** → **Data cache** → **Route cache** → **Revalidation considerations**. Add branches where the learner must choose an alternative and label the evidence for the choice.

choices.feedback: Request memoization is best understood as follows: Request memoization deduplicates identical data work within one render request but does not necessarily persist across requests.
choices.label: Request memoization deduplicates identical data work within one render request but does not necessarily persist across requests.

choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Request memoization.
choices.label: The framework coordinates routing, rendering, bundling, caching, and deployment, while the application still owns domain and security decisions.

choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Request memoization.
choices.label: React Server Components serialize server-rendered results, client-component references, and props into an RSC payload.

sections.body: Request memoization deduplicates identical data work within one render request but does not necessarily persist across requests.

summary: How Next.js Caches Data connects Request memoization, Data cache, Route cache.
Use the topic to make behavior and decisions explicit rather than memorizing isolated syntax.

topicQuiz.failurePolicy: Show explanations, add missed objectives to local review, and allow immediate or later retry.

whyThisMatters: The ideas in how next.js caches data recur throughout later Next.js work, reducing memorization and debugging later. It belongs to the **Streaming, Rendering Strategies, and Caching** module.
